QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_4

question.text: What is the typical face value of a mutual fund unit in India when a new scheme is launched?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When a new mutual fund scheme is launched in India (during the New Fund Offer or NFO), each unit is typically issued at a face value of Rs. 10. After the NFO, the NAV changes daily based on the performance of the underlying investments. **The Trap:** Rs. 100 is the face value of most shares in the stock market (though this varies), not mutual fund units. Do not confuse the two. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under NFO and unit pricing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When a client sees a fund with NAV of Rs. 50, it does not mean the fund is "expensive." It simply means the fund has grown from its initial Rs. 10 to Rs. 50 over time. A higher NAV reflects past growth, not future cost.
